reviewer_notes: Manual taint-flow analysis of all 244 PHP files. Two automated findings reviewed; both are false positives. No confirmed exploitable vulnerabilities found. Plugin demonstrates strong security posture: nonce verification on all state-changing operations, capability checks (loco_admin) enforced in base Controller::auth(), no unauthenticated AJAX or REST endpoints, and file path normalization with directory boundary checks on all file operations.

findings.reasoning: The flagged pattern `wp_redirect($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'])` is a standard POST-redirect-GET pattern. REQUEST_URI contains only the path component of the current URL (e.g., '/wp-admin/admin.php?page=...'), never an external scheme+host. An attacker cannot control REQUEST_URI to redirect to an arbitrary external URL via this code path. WordPress's own wp_redirect() additionally sanitizes the location. Furthermore, this code is only reachable after a valid nonce check (checkNonce) and the user must be logged in with the 'loco_admin' capability. Not exploitable as an open redirect.

The Loco_data_Cookie class is explicitly marked '@codeCoverageIgnore' with a PHPDoc note stating 'Not currently used anywhere - replaced with usermeta-based session'. A full codebase grep confirms the class is referenced only in its own definition and in test/WordPressTestCase.php (test helper only — not production). The class is dead code and has no callers in production code paths. parse_str writes into a local $data variable with a second argument (not extract()), so even if reached, variables would be isolated to that local scope. No variable injection is possible.

entry_points_reviewed: All admin page controllers (AdminRouter routes: root, bundle, file, config, list, init)
All AJAX controllers (loco_ajax / loco_download actions): Save, Upload, Download, Sync, Msginit, Xgettext, FsConnect, FsReference, Apis, Diff, DownloadConf, Ping
attack_surface_summary.auth_model: All admin controllers inherit from Loco_mvc_Controller which calls auth() requiring is_user_logged_in() AND current_user_can('loco_admin'). All AJAX controllers call validate() which enforces nonce check via checkNonce(). No wp_ajax_nopriv handlers found. No REST API endpoints.
attack_surface_summary.file_operations: All file read/write operations go through Loco_fs_File with normalize() path resolution and are constrained to WP_CONTENT_DIR. FsReferenceController additionally enforces underContentDirectory() || underWordPressDirectory() and blocks wp-config.php explicitly.
notable_non_issues: FsReferenceController source viewer: well-gated behind code_view setting, capability check, path boundary check, extension allowlist, and translatable-string content validation
UploadController: only accepts .po/.mo extensions, validates locale extraction, enforces correct filename, goes through Loco_api_WordPressFileSystem authorization
SaveController: validates file extension to po/pot/mo only, normalizes path against WP_CONTENT_DIR
Cookie class: dead code, never instantiated in production
REQUEST_URI usage: only for same-page PRG pattern, not redirectable to external URLs
